Sparse Attention via Entmax for Forecasting

Smith, A. (2024)

Abstract

We propose using entmax sparse transformations in temporal attention layers to achieve interpretable covariate selection in time series forecasting models. Unlike softmax, entmax concentrates probability mass on a small number of active covariates, producing sparse attention distributions where irrelevant inputs receive exactly zero weight.

1. Introduction

Forecasting with many covariates requires an attention mechanism that can distinguish relevant from irrelevant features. Classical softmax attention assigns positive weight to every input, making interpretation difficult.

2. Method

We use alpha-entmax with alpha=1.5 as a drop-in replacement for softmax in the attention module. The resulting sparse distributions are differentiable and well-suited to gradient-based optimisation.

3. Experiments

On the M5 benchmark, entmax attention reduces the active covariate set by 60% while maintaining forecast accuracy within 0.5% of the full-softmax baseline.
